rows.label: Retake rules
rows.cat: Once per year — one shot per season
rows.gre: Every 21 days, up to 5 per 12 months

rows.label: Loan implication
rows.gre: MS ₹30–60L; Germany/funded-US routes can be near-zero

rows.label: The category math
rows.cat: Shortlist cutoffs vary by category-gender-background pool — public policy PDFs

choose_if.cat: Choose CAT if: budget ≤ ₹50L is hard, your pool math is favorable (check the current policy PDF, not folklore), and you can commit 15+ hrs/week for one season.
choose_if.gmat: Choose GMAT if: you want ISB or a global MBA, you value a retakeable exam with a 5-year shelf life, and financing ₹60L+ is realistic for you.
choose_if.gre: Choose GRE if: MS/MEM is on your list at all — it keeps the most doors open per rupee of prep, including a later MBA pivot.

facts.claim: CAT registration for General category has been ~₹2,600 (recent cycles).
GMAT: 5 attempts per rolling 12 months, 8 lifetime; score valid 5 years.
GRE: retake every 21 days, max 5 per 12 months; score valid 5 years.
